mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market and expanding economy, which typically translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand and potential economic recession, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate revenues decline.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where the RSAFS trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is low and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is rising, it indicates an overheating economy prompting aggressive monetary tightening, which typically precedes negative S&P 500 forward returns due to increased borrowing costs and reduced liquidity.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 6% AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.1% in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances, or the combined signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Robust real GDP growth (GDPC1) reflects a strong and expanding economy, which underpins corporate profitability and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive correlation with GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth during periods of economic expansion.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and impending economic contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate outlooks worsen.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances following a triggered increase in UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: Consistently low consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) allows central banks to maintain accommodative monetary policy, which supports asset valuations and encourages risk-taking, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns do not show a positive correlation with low CPIAUCSL growth (e.g., below 2% annualized) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates a broad-based contraction across manufacturing and construction sectors, signaling a severe economic downturn and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive or flat in more than 40% of instances where both industrial production and housing starts are contracting.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) maintained at near-zero levels signifies highly accommodative monetary policy, which reduces the cost of capital and encourages investment, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns, particularly during economic recovery phases.
evidence_refs: fact:Historically low interest rates support equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.
